bridge_fastener_policy.standard: DIN 965 / ISO 7046 countersunk flat head screw
bridge_fastener_policy.source: supplier DIN 965 / ISO 7046 M1.4 countersunk screw dimensions
bridge_fastener_policy.basis: bridge plate thickness is driven by countersink depth plus residual material; remaining height below bridge bottom is split 2/3 support face and 1/3 service step

source_basis: watchmaking pivots are commonly sub-0.2 mm; visible demo arbors use larger body radii
center arbor is intentionally larger than later train arbors
barrel arbor is largest because it anchors the energy-source package

screw_policy.basis: reserve an outer service band for future bridge screw pads and raised standoffs before bridge CAD is generated
screw_policy.note: M1.4 DIN 84-style screws have about 2.6 mm head diameter and would require a much wider band and a new solver pass.

outer_raised_support_ring.bridge_fastener_standard: DIN 965 / ISO 7046 countersunk flat head screw
